title: Nigerian president's rivals get major boost as political heavyweight joins fold
content: Rabiu Kwankwaso joins the ADC, a coalition poised to take on President Tinubu in the 2027 elections.

originalTitle: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
title: A Sad Day in Ghana: Learning About Safe Buildings
content:
Hello, my young friends! Today, we have some news from a beautiful country in Africa called Ghana. It's a bit sad, but it's important to talk about it so we can learn and grow together.

A Sad Day in Ghana

Once upon a time, not long ago, in a town in Ghana, something happened that made many people sad. It was a day when a building fell down. This building was not fully finished yet. It was still like a big puzzle that wasn't put together all the way. Imagine building a toy house, but you haven't put the roof on, or some walls are still missing. That's a bit like how this building was. But even though it wasn't finished, some people were using it. They were inside for a church service. A church service is when people go to pray, sing happy songs, and listen to stories together. Did you know? Ghana is famous for its delicious cocoa beans, which are used to make yummy chocolate! It's a warm and friendly country in West Africa.

What Happened?

This unfinished building was in a school area. Schools are very special places where children like you go to learn, play, and make friends. It's where you grow your mind and heart. One day, while people were inside the unfinished building, it just collapsed. That means it fell down suddenly, like a stack of blocks tumbling over very fast. It made a very big noise and lots of dust filled the air. Everyone inside and nearby was scared and worried. It was a very frightening moment for many people. It must have been a very confusing and loud sound. Sadly, some people got hurt because of the falling building. And a few grown-ups lost their lives. This made everyone in the community and all over Ghana feel very, very sad for them and their families. Did you know? Buildings need strong foundations, like the deep roots of a big tree, to stand tall and safe for a very long time! Without strong roots, a tree might fall.

Helping Hands Came Quickly

But guess what? Even in very sad times, people in Africa are very kind, brave, and helpful. They come together like a big family to support each other. As soon as the building fell, many brave people rushed to help. These were neighbors, friends, and special helpers called rescue workers. They are like superheroes who help people in danger. They worked very hard, digging through the fallen parts and broken pieces. Their job was to find people who needed help and make sure everyone was safe and sound. They carefully brought people out and took them to doctors to make them feel better if they were hurt. It showed how much people care for each other in Ghana. Did you know? When people help each other in tough times, it's called community spirit. Africans have a very strong community spirit, which means they look out for one another!

What We Are Learning

This sad event teaches us something very important. All buildings, no matter how big or small, must be very, very strong and safe. Safety is the most important thing for everyone. They need special people called engineers to plan them out carefully. Engineers are like super-builders who know all the secrets to making things stand up straight and strong for many years. An unfinished building might not be safe to use. It's like trying to eat a cake that's not fully baked – it's just not ready yet! And it's not good to eat until it's properly finished and cooked. Everyone who builds something needs to follow important rules. These rules are there to make sure that the building is strong and safe for people to be inside, whether they are playing, learning, or praying. Did you know? In many African countries, people often build their homes and help their neighbors build theirs, showing teamwork and togetherness!

Let's Build Safe Together

Our big wish is that all buildings in Ghana, and all over Africa, are super safe for everyone. We want every child and every grown-up to feel safe when they are inside a building. This means that we must always make sure buildings are properly finished. They also need to be checked very carefully by experts before anyone uses them. This is like checking your bicycle's brakes before you ride it. When buildings are strong and safe, people can learn, play, worship, and live without worry. This helps communities grow happy and healthy. It's a promise that everyone can work together to make sure our schools, churches, and homes are the safest places they can be for all our friends and families. Did you know? The tallest building in all of Africa is called The Leonardo, and it's in South Africa! It's super tall and super strong, built by amazing engineers.

Our African Family

Even though this was a sad story, it shows how much people care for each other in Ghana and all over Africa. When something bad happens, we all come together like a big family. We send our love, our kind thoughts, and our prayers to the people in Ghana who are sad right now. We wish them peace and strength during this difficult time. We hope that soon, they will feel better, and new, very safe places will be built for them. Places where everyone can feel happy and secure again. Let's all remember to be kind, helpful, and to always think about safety first. We are all one big African family, caring for each other. Did you know? The word "Ubuntu" in some African languages means "I am because we are." It teaches us that we are all connected and important to each other!
summary: An unfinished building in Ghana fell down during a church service, making people sad, but brave helpers came to make sure everyone was safe, teaching us that buildings must always be strong and secure.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us how super important it is for all buildings to be strong and finished so everyone can be safe when they are inside, whether for school, church, or just to live.

originalTitle: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
title: DR Congo's Big Dream: Playing in the World Cup!
content:
Hello, my super friends across Africa and the world! It's your friendly news reporter here, ready to share some very exciting news from the heart of our amazing continent. Today, we're going to talk about a big country called DR Congo. Have you heard of it? It's a wonderful place with lots of kind people and beautiful nature!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine a giant, super-fun party where all the best football teams from different countries come to play. That's kind of what the World Cup is! It's a very special football tournament. Teams play to find out who is the best in the whole wide world. It happens only once every four years, which is a long, long wait! **Did you know?** Football is also called soccer in some places! It's the most popular sport in the world, played with a round ball and two goals. All the players work very hard, practicing every day, to be super good at kicking the ball and scoring goals. It's a very exciting game to watch!

DR Congo's Big Hope!

Our friends from DR Congo have a very big dream. They want their national football team to go to this amazing World Cup party! It's a huge honour for any country to send its team. When your team plays in the World Cup, the whole country watches and cheers together. Right now, the DR Congo team is playing a super important game. They are playing against a team from a country called Jamaica. Jamaica is an island far away, known for its beautiful beaches and fast runners! This game is like a final test. If DR Congo wins, they get to go to the World Cup! Isn't that exciting?

A Long, Long Wait

Do you know how long DR Congo has been waiting to go to the World Cup again? It's been 52 years! That's a very, very long time. Imagine waiting for more than 50 birthdays! That's how many years it's been since their team last played in the World Cup. That was way back in 1974. For many of us, that was even before our parents or grandparents were born! It shows how much this means to the people of DR Congo. **Did you know?** The number 52 is bigger than all the fingers and toes you have! It's a big number for years to wait. They want to show everyone how good their players are and how much they love football. This game against Jamaica is their chance!

Who are the DR Congo Team?

The DR Congo team is also known by a cool nickname: 'The Leopards'! Leopards are strong, fast, and very smart animals, just like the players on the team. These players have worked so hard. They have run many miles, kicked thousands of balls, and trained in all kinds of weather. They want to make their country proud. When they play, they don't just play for themselves. They play for every child, every mum, every dad, and every grandma and grandpa in DR Congo. They play for the whole nation's joy!

The Big Match Against Jamaica

The game against Jamaica is called a 'play-off' game. It means it's one of the last games to decide who gets to go to the big World Cup tournament. It's like the final hurdle before the finish line. Both teams will try their very best to win. They will run, tackle, pass, and shoot with all their might! Imagine everyone in DR Congo gathered around their TVs or radios, cheering loudly for 'The Leopards'! The air will be full of excitement and hope. **Did you know?** Fans wear special clothes and paint their faces with their country's colours to show support for their team! Every kick, every save, every goal will be met with cheers and claps. It's a moment when everyone feels connected, like one big family.

More Than Just a Game

This game is more than just kicking a ball around. It's about hope. It's about showing the world how strong and talented the people of DR Congo are. If they win, it will bring so much happiness and pride. It will inspire young boys and girls in DR Congo to chase their own dreams, just like 'The Leopards' are chasing theirs. It tells everyone that with hard work and determination, big dreams can come true. It shows that even after a long wait, it's always possible to reach for the stars. We are all cheering for DR Congo! We hope 'The Leopards' play their very best game and make history for their country. Go, DR Congo, go! We wish them all the best in their super important game. No matter what, they are already heroes for trying so hard! Keep playing, keep dreaming!
summary: DR Congo's football team, 'The Leopards,' is playing a big game against Jamaica to try and go to the World Cup for the first time in 52 years, bringing much hope and excitement to their country.
whyMatters: This game is very important because if DR Congo wins, their football team will play in the big World Cup, which will make everyone in their country very happy and proud after waiting for so many years, showing that big dreams can come true.

originalTitle: US airport lines shorten as TSA workers get paid
title: US airport lines shorten as TSA workers get paid
content: Airports like JFK and Houston report shorter wait times, though LaGuardia still faces delays of up to two hours.

originalTitle: Two more UN peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon: UNIFIL
title: Sad News About Our Brave Peace Helpers
content:
Jambo, my wonderful friends! Your friend from Africa is here with some news. Sometimes news can be a little bit sad, but it's important to understand what's happening in our big, wide world.

Our Brave Peace Helpers

Imagine there are very special people in the world. They wear blue helmets and blue vests. They go to places where people are having big arguments or even fighting. These special people are called "peacekeepers." Their job is to help everyone calm down and be safe. They are like superheroes, but instead of flying, they bring peace! They work for a very important group called the United Nations. This is like a very big club where almost all the countries in the world are members. They all work together to make the world a better, safer place for everyone.

What Happened Far Away

Sometimes, even when peacekeepers are trying their very best, things can be tricky. There is a country far away from us, called Lebanon. It's a beautiful place, but sometimes the people there have big fights. Our brave peacekeepers from the United Nations go there to help. Sadly, this week, some of these brave peacekeepers got hurt while they were working to keep the peace in Lebanon. We heard that three of them, our wonderful helpers, are no longer with us. This makes us feel very sad, because they were trying to do something very good for everyone. Did you know? Peacekeepers come from all over the world, even from countries in Africa! They leave their homes to help others they don't even know.

Who Are Peacekeepers?

Let's think more about these amazing people. Peacekeepers are like a big team of grown-ups who want to help. They might help children go to school safely. They might help doctors and nurses take care of sick people. They might even help make sure everyone has enough food and water. Their main job is to try and stop fights. They stand between people who are arguing to try and make them stop. They remind everyone that it's much better to be friends and live in peace. They don't carry big weapons to fight, but they carry kindness and hope.

Where is Lebanon?

Lebanon is a country far, far away from Africa. If you look at a map, it's in a part of the world called the Middle East. It has beautiful mountains and a lovely sea. But like any place, sometimes the people there face problems. The United Nations has sent peacekeepers to Lebanon for many, many years. They have a special name there, called UNIFIL. It's a long word, but it just means the United Nations people who are keeping the peace in Lebanon. They have been there for a long time, trying to help people live together without fighting. Did you know? The UN has sent peacekeepers to many, many different countries around the world, helping millions of people!

Why Are They There?

Sometimes, when grown-ups in different groups or countries have a very big disagreement, they start to fight. This fighting can make many people sad and scared. It can make homes unsafe and stop children from playing outside. The peacekeepers go there to try and make things better. They talk to the people fighting. They try to find ways for everyone to agree without hurting each other. They watch over the areas to make sure new fights don't start. They are like very patient teachers trying to help everyone share their toys nicely.

The United Nations Family

The United Nations is like a super big family of countries. They all meet to talk about how to solve big problems in the world, like stopping wars, helping people who are hungry, and making sure everyone has a chance to learn. When peacekeepers go to help, it's the United Nations that sends them. They wear the UN flag, which is a picture of the world in blue and white. It shows that they are there for everyone, not just one country. Did you know? The United Nations was started a long, long time ago, after a very big war, so that countries could talk instead of fight!

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still think about our brave peacekeepers and the people in Lebanon. We can remember that kindness is very important. In our own homes and schools, we can try our best to be peacekeepers too! We can share our toys, use kind words, and try to make up with our friends if we have a little argument. Every little act of kindness helps make the world a more peaceful place. Let's send our love and thoughts to the families of these brave peacekeepers who helped others. They will always be remembered as heroes who tried to make the world a better place for children everywhere. We hope for peace in Lebanon and all over the world.
summary: Brave United Nations peacekeepers, who help people far away in Lebanon, sadly got hurt this week while working to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: It's important because it shows us that brave people help others in faraway places to stop fights, and we can all help by being kind and peaceful too.

originalTitle: How Sky can ignore limit on its TV picks & why fans are fed up
title: Football Fun Changes Times! What's Going On?
content:
Hello Super Football Fans!

Hey there, super football fans! Are you ready for some exciting news? We all love football, right? It's the best game ever! Kicking the ball, scoring goals, cheering for our favourite teams! It's so much fun to watch. Many of you love watching the big football games from far away. You wake up early or stay up late. You want to see your favourite teams play. This news is about those super big games. It is about how some games change their times. Let's find out more together!

What is Premier League Football?

Do you know about the Premier League? It is a very big football league. It happens far away in England. Many famous teams play in the Premier League. Teams like Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal, and Liverpool. Maybe you have a favourite team from there! These teams play against each other all year. They want to win the big trophy! Millions of people watch these games. They watch from all over the world. Many, many fans in Africa love the Premier League too. You cheer for your favourite players! **Did you know?** The Premier League is one of the most-watched sports leagues in the world! People in over 200 countries watch it! That's a lot of TVs tuned in!

Watching Football on TV!

How do we watch these amazing games? We watch them on TV, of course! TV helps us see games happening very far away. It brings the stadium right into our homes. We can see every kick and every goal. It feels like we are right there! We sit with our families and friends. We share snacks and shout when our team scores. TV makes it possible to enjoy football from anywhere. It is a magical way to see sports. We are so lucky to have TV!

Sometimes Games Change Time!

Now, here is the tricky part. Sometimes, the big football games change their schedule. This means a game that was supposed to be on Saturday afternoon might move. It might move to Friday night. Or it might move to Monday night. Imagine that! You plan to watch a game on Saturday. But then you find out it moved! This can make things a bit tricky for fans. It's like planning a special dinner, and then the time changes. Oops!

Why Do Games Move? Meet Sky TV!

So, why do these games move? There is a big reason. It is because of TV companies! One very big TV company is called Sky. Sky pays a lot of money. They pay money to the Premier League. They pay so they can show the games live on TV. Sky wants to show the best games at the best times. They want many, many people to watch. When more people watch, more people like Sky TV. So, Sky gets to pick some games. They can choose to move them to Friday or Monday. They want to show exciting games when lots of eyes are on the TV! It is all about getting many viewers. **Did you know?** TV companies pay billions of dollars to show Premier League games! That's more money than you can count! This money helps the football clubs too.

What is a "Headache" for Fans?

The news said this is a "headache" for fans. What does that mean? It doesn't mean their head actually hurts! It means it's a big problem. Imagine you live in England. You bought a ticket to see your favourite team. The game was on Saturday. You planned your trip. You booked a train ticket. You told your friends. But then the game moves to Friday night! Now your train ticket is wrong. You might miss school or work to go. It is very difficult to change plans. It causes a lot of trouble. This is a headache!

A "Headache" for Fans in Africa Too!

This "headache" can happen for us in Africa too! Even if we don't go to the stadium. Why? Because of time! When games move to Friday or Monday, the time can be very tricky for us. A game on Friday night in England might be late, late at night here. Or a game on Monday night might be very early on Tuesday morning! You might want to watch it before school. But that means waking up super, super early! Or staying up very late on a school night. Your parents might not like that! It can be hard to watch your team when the times keep changing. **Did you know?** Africa has many different time zones! For example, when it's 3 PM in England, it might be 4 PM in South Africa, 5 PM in Kenya, or 2 PM in Ghana! This makes watching games even more interesting!

Is it Fair for Everyone?

Now, the big question: Is this fair for everyone? Some people say it is fair. They say Sky TV pays a lot of money. This money helps the Premier League and the football clubs. It helps them buy good players. It helps them have nice stadiums. So, Sky should get to pick the best times. It helps more people watch the games. But other people say it is not fair. They say it is not fair for the fans. Especially for the fans who want to go to the stadium. It makes it very hard for them. It costs them more money and time. It also makes it tricky for fans like us in Africa. We just want to watch our team play without missing sleep or school! It's a tricky balance.

What Can Fans Do?

So, what can fans do? It is hard to change big plans made by TV companies. But fans can talk about it! They can write letters. They can talk on social media (with their parents' help, of course!). They can tell people that the changing times are difficult. Sometimes, when many people talk, big companies listen. It's important to share how you feel. Because football is for the fans! We love football!

Keep Loving Football, Super Fans!

No matter when the games are, we will always love football! It is a game that brings us together. It teaches us about teamwork and trying our best. Even with these tricky time changes, our love for the game stays strong. Keep cheering for your teams! Keep playing football with your friends! And keep learning about the amazing world of football. You are the best fans in the world! Hooray for football!
summary: Premier League football games sometimes change their times because TV companies like Sky want to show them when many people can watch, but this makes it tricky for fans in England to go to games and for kids in Africa to watch on TV without missing sleep or school.
whyMatters: It's important because many kids love football, and when games change times, it can be hard to watch our favourite teams, so we should understand why this happens and think about what is fair for all fans.

originalTitle: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
title: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
content: Uefa is to expected to broadly freeze prices at Euro 2028 - meaning supporters would be able to buy five tickets for the cost of one parking space at a World Cup stadium this summer.
